fragments.content: Type 2 diabetes mellitus (T2DM) is a chronic metabolic disorder characterized by insulin resistance and relative insulin deficiency, leading to hyperglycemia. Pathophysiology involves progressive beta-cell dysfunction alongside peripheral insulin resistance in muscle, liver, and adipose tissue. Genetic predisposition combined with environmental factors (obesity, sedentary lifestyle, aging) drives disease development. Diagnostic criteria: HbA1c ≥6.5%, fasting plasma glucose ≥126 mg/dL, 2-hour OGTT ≥200 mg/dL, or random glucose ≥200 mg/dL with symptoms. T2DM accounts for 90-95% of all diabetes cases. Chronic hyperglycemia leads to microvascular (retinopathy, nephropathy, neuropathy) and macrovascular (coronary artery disease, stroke, peripheral arterial disease) complications.

Treatment algorithm for T2DM: First-line therapy is metformin (500-2000 mg/day) unless contraindicated. For patients with established ASCVD, heart failure, or CKD, initiate GLP-1 receptor agonist (semaglutide, liraglutide, dulaglutide) or SGLT2 inhibitor (empagliflozin, dapagliflozin, canagliflozin) regardless of HbA1c or metformin use. If additional glycemic control needed, consider combination therapy based on comorbidities: GLP-1 RA preferred for weight loss and ASCVD risk reduction; SGLT2i for heart failure and CKD; DPP-4 inhibitors as weight-neutral option; sulfonylureas as cost-effective but hypoglycemia risk; TZDs effective but fluid retention concerns. Insulin therapy indicated when HbA1c >10%, symptomatic hyperglycemia, or failure of multiple oral agents. Target HbA1c <7% for most adults, individualized based on age, comorbidities, hypoglycemia risk.

Diagnostic criteria for T2DM: Any one of the following confirms diagnosis: (1) HbA1c ≥6.5% using NGSP-certified method; (2) Fasting plasma glucose ≥126 mg/dL (≥7.0 mmol/L) after ≥8-hour fast; (3) 2-hour plasma glucose ≥200 mg/dL during 75g OGTT; (4) Random plasma glucose ≥200 mg/dL with classic hyperglycemia symptoms. Abnormal results should be confirmed with repeat testing unless unequivocal hyperglycemia present. Prediabetes defined as HbA1c 5.7-6.4%, fasting glucose 100-125 mg/dL, or 2-hour OGTT 140-199 mg/dL. Screening recommended for adults ≥35 years, or younger with BMI ≥25 kg/m² plus risk factors (family history, hypertension, dyslipidemia, physical inactivity, high-risk ethnicity, history of gestational diabetes). Annual screening for prediabetes; every 3 years if normal.
